measurement_focus.analysis_notes: Optimize for recorded in-app subscriptions first. Use app installs as the secondary fallback when subscription volume is sparse or revenue reporting is still stabilizing.

accounts.notes: Replace with the real Meta ad account ID before using sync-api.

measurement_focus.analysis_notes: Optimize for purchases first. Use outbound clicks as a diagnostic secondary signal for under-attributed creatives, not as a replacement for purchase efficiency.

action_policy.guardrail_notes: CUTOFF = 1.8 ROAS: if an ad has been running more than a couple of days (past evaluation_grace_days) and shows no other positive signals (improving trajectory, strong hook rate, etc.), pause it when blended ROAS is at or below 1.8. DO NOT apply the cutoff to a brand-new ad in its first 1-2 days, or to any ad right after a BIG change (creative/audience/budget) — give it up to evaluation_grace_days to stabilize. NEVER pause on the cutoff if the ad has barely spent (< min_spend_before_pause): that is insufficient data, so keep it running rather than calling it a loser.

accounts.notes: Replace with the real Meta ad account ID before using sync-api.

measurement_focus.analysis_notes: Lead generation via Meta Instant Forms (objective OUTCOME_LEADS). Success = form leads; efficiency = cost per lead (CPR). This account does NOT sell products — do not apply purchases, purchase value, or ROAS to it. VERIFY primary_result_action_type on first sync-api run (instant-form lead action type may resolve as 'leadgen_grouped' or 'onsite_conversion.lead_grouped'); if Results come back blank, let the sync infer or adjust here.

action_policy.guardrail_notes: GOAL = $10 per lead (target_cost_per_result). CUTOFF = $40 per lead (pause_cost_per_result_above): if an ad has run more than a couple of days (past evaluation_grace_days) with no other positive signals, pause it when cost-per-lead is at or above $40. DO NOT apply the cutoff in a new ad's first 1-2 days, or right after a BIG change — give it up to evaluation_grace_days. NEVER pause on the cutoff if the ad has barely spent (< min_spend_before_pause): insufficient data, keep running. ROAS/purchase metrics do not apply to this account.

accounts.notes: Currently PAUSED (no spend in the last 30 days). ~$98k spent and 12,600+ form leads across 2024-2025 at roughly $25-30 cost-per-lead historically. Instant Forms capture leads on-platform, so there is no website destination or offsite-checkout requirement for this account.
